target.name: Tekever
target.founder: Ricardo Mendes, Vitor Cristina, Pedro Sinogas

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $631M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Percepto
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

private.name: Monarch Tractor
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Tekever, a Series C-stage drone and autonomous systems company that has raised $631M+ to date, is evaluated within a sector containing six private peers (Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, Percepto) and no direct public comparables. The narrow peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or valuation-tier density to support reliable median multiples.

Stage-progression analysis, the primary applicable method, applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $631M capital raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $1.1B. This method treats total raised as a floor-level valuation signal and applies standard venture step-up expectations to estimate post-financing enterprise value. The peer-median approach was not applied: while four same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints, making sector-wide median analysis unreliable and potentially misleading.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $912M (low) to $1.2B (high), centered on $1.1B. This range reflects the stage-progression result and a reasonable variance band around it. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 1.7x multiplier for Tekever's specific risk profile and market conditions, and the lack of transaction or earnings multiples from comparable-stage peers. Additional revenue-based or margin-adjusted analysis would require operational metrics not present in available data.
